label: Experiencing Lake Superior's Rocks and Shores

comments.text: A pile of rocks that always fascinated me in Lake Ontario. Caught at the right time, they look pretty amazing! #Photography #EastCoastKin #longexposure

Found mostly around Lake Superior, these glowing rocks are known as Yooperlites. They are actually syanite rocks rich in fluorescent sodalite - and were only discovered recently! More about Yooperlites and how to find them👇
earthlymission.com/yooperlites-... 📷 Tarah Nicole Hoffman

Hunting for Yooperlites on a Lake Superior beach Yooperlite is a rare luminescent soapstone that glows under ultraviolet light, like a black light. #rockintuesday #tidesouttuesday #coloraday #bluetue #eastcoastkin #artyear #photography

Dig your toes into the sand, hear the gentle waves reaching up onto shore. Spend hours looking for pretty rocks and fossils. A gift of peace and calm from me to you. #pleinair #pleinairpainting #lakemichigan #fineart #oilpainting #beach
My dad gave me all my sister’s rocks from Lake Superior, it’s almost like being there! P.S: Don’t tell my sister 😆
Fluorescent rock hunting on Lake Erie. 🔦 Hello fellow Rockhounds! #rockhound #naturelover #nature #explore #geology
At the waters edge of Lake Superior. Looking for rocks. Breathing in the fresh lake air

What scientific discovery absolutely blew you away? Of course there are many for me, but this one comes to mind (one I discuss in ENDS OF THE EARTH): freshwater lakes, some the size of the Great Lakes, buried under 2 or more miles of ice in Antarctica. With living creatures inside, no less.
Hollow Rock as day turns to night on Lake Superior
Finlay looking out onto Lake Superior on #RockinTuesday

20 years ago I stood on the granite bedrock that underpins the north shore of Lake Superior and learned my father had died. #photography #LakeSuperior #rock #death #EastCoastKin #BlueSkyArtShow #TheJourney

A whole lotta rocks on the shores of Lake Erie These rocks are up to 400 million years old, and are from the carving out of the lake basin by rivers and glaciers! #RockinTuesday #TidesOutTuesday #EastCoastKin #lakescape #waterscape #landscape #photography #photographersofbluesky
#LakeErie #Ohio

One more #RockinTuesday post. I found the rock in the second image below on a Lake Superior beach. I have omars, like the ones in the first picture, but the hole in the second rock is deeper and looks like it was made by hand, maybe for a tool? I could be way off on this. Any opinions? Other ideas?

Apparently it's #RockinTuesday and I have too many rock pix. So here's a video of my favourite beach, where you can walk on 200 million year old fossils. #fossils #GreyCounty #rocks #geology #LakeHuron
#RockinTuesday Rocks under water in clear Lake Superior water to bring out their color.

Super-Size That?
A friend thought this might have been shot somewhere on the Oregon coast, that big rock 40' or 50' tall, a mighty surf curling in from the Pacific. But no. It's the Wisconsin coast and the big rock is 4' or 5' tall on a nice November day.
#GreatLakes #LakeMichigan #photography

Rock hunting through the crystal clear water of Lake Ontario. #RockinTuesday #LakeOntario #BeachRocks #photography #ECK #EastCoastKin

i'm like a little ADHD gremlin. I was just unpacking and checked the pockets of the shorts I wore this weekend and found rocks from the beach on Lake Superior. I'd completely forgotten that I picked them up.

7. Yooperlites, collected from Lake Superior in Michigan. These little cuties look boring under normal light, but when hit with a UV light, the sodalite within them exhibits intense fluorescence. This is how you find them - by slowly scanning beaches at night with a UV light!

For today’s #ThingThursday I have a bottle of agates in oil of some kind and stoppered with cork that were collected from the North Shore of Lake Superior in the late 1910s by my grandmother when she went to visit her brother, when he was working as a lighthouse keeper. It’s one of my top treasures.
A small rock on a really big lake. Lake Superior, Minnesota

Hand-polished a rock for the first time, this pretty guy from Lake Ontario. Still have the aluminum oxide step to do, but I ran out of time. Also forgot to take a "before" picture but wow was this ever a satisfying process !
Lake Michigan crashing into Painted Rock's cliffs: #AGoodPlace Source: www.reddit.com/r/Damnthatsi...
OK, Last Pic tonight of some rocks from Lake Superior, UP! #rockhound
This made me laugh because I, too, carry a large rock in my purse. (What! It's a good rock! It's from Lake Superior and is over a billion years old. I'd be stupid NOT to carry it)

Black Rocks in Presque Isle Park, Marquette, Michigan, USA. People cliff dive off these rocks into Lake Superior. Me? No. But People do. #rockintuesday #artyear #art #artist #photography #photo #hike #hiking #travel #upperpeninsula #smittenwiththemitten

I created this map to help Rockhounds find fluorescent sodalite🔦 in the Great Lakes. There are over 70 locations on the map from Wisconsin to NY. #map #rockhound #explore #nature #geology EXPLORE MORE! NORTH AMERICA: Great Lakes Fluorescent Sodalite Map maps.app.goo.gl/Ft2dCMoyzUuB...

Last fall we traveled to Grand Marais Michigan to look for #yooperlites on the #LakeSuperior shoreline. Fun trip! #rockhounding
#rocks
#RockinTuesday A big THANK YOU to whoever thought this one up. Here is another image—wet rocks on a Great Lakes shore with a small trespasser.
#RockinTuesday Mine, just plain old rocks on Lake Superior with people sitting on them, but I wish I were sitting there now. Have a great week.

Another #RockinTuesday image. Sun-dappled rocks in crystal-clear Lake Superior water. I tried to get a simpler word than "dappled" for this, and "stippled" came up. This is why writers pull their hair out.😁 Have a sun-dappled/stippled day.

These glowing rocks are called yooperlites. Packed with fluorescent sodalite, and found mostly on the shores of Lake Superior, these stones grow orange when exposed to UV Light. During my hike in the UP, I found a few right along the beaches. #hiking #picturedrocks #photography
The raw Lake Ontario death plate, we'll see if I can do anything with it #fossils are so cool

Last year, I designed ceramic #GreatLakes #map coasters for my brother, replacing his older (and inaccurate) ones. If you want some, send me a DM or email! They are $10/each, plus shipping cost. For now, I'm not listing them on my store page, just selling them here via word of mouth.
#RockinTuesday A toad, trying to pretend he's a wet rock on a Lake Superior beach full of rocks.

These miniature carvings bring me joy! From top center: Gale Crater, Lake Superior bathymetry, Grand Canyon South Rim, Holden Crater, Palikir Crater, and a random Martian crater in the center. Made from resin with some small bits of wood thrown in. #artAdventCalendar
woodgrainterrain.com

#RockinTuesday Still and always, my favorite among the "hashtag" days. Mine, a rock "sculpture" built on a Great Lakes beach by my son when he was pretty small. He was always one of those kids you had to keep an eye on . . .😁

FossilFriday!
A picture I took at Graycliff, a summer home designed by Frank Lloyd Wright along the shore of Lake Erie. This is a closeup of the fireplace in the main room. The igneous and metamorphic rocks are from Canada and were carried down by glacial ice & there is a brachiopod popped in there!

No joke, I have an app called Rock Identifier, and it rules. I got it when we went to Lake Superior this summer. Boom, instant way to keep the kids occupied for hours IDing and learning about rocks on the shoreline. You just take a picture of your rock, and it tells you everything you want to know.
This is tumbled from the great lakes. It is botryoidal chalcedony in chert. These are my favorite rocks to collect, and are very similar to quartz.
Showing my husband a cool rock that I found 🪨 #geology #lakesuperior #northshore #agate #rocks #rockhunting #superiorhikingtrail #gooseberryfalls #hiking #midwest #lake #minnesota #hike #firstpost
Found mostly around Lake Superior, these glowing rocks are known as Yooperlites. They are actually syanite rocks rich in fluorescent sodalite - and were only discovered recently!

The Pink pebbles and rocks of Iona's Beach on the North Shore of Lake Superior in Minnesota are a perfect choice for #Stunday. In summer you might even be lucky enough to find wild berries growing along the edge of the beach and pathways. #scientific
#pink
#lakesuperior
#minnesota

Rocks. The red one is from the north shore of Lake Superior near the Brule River, the green one is from the south shore of Superior between Black River and Presque Isle River. Old, old Laurentian Shield rocks. No polish - just years of me handling them. Good for traveling through time and space.
Told y’all I went looking for rocks today. Very peaceful day getting nothing done. And sent to see #Sinners, highly recommend 4.5/5⭐️ #LakeOntario #ChimneyBluffs #Kingfisher #Rocks #WNY

I used to live beside the waves. When I was small, I would clamber all over the boulders beside Lake Superior, looking in the cracks for secrets. I only ever found trash, more rocks, and once a wiffle ball I set in a place of honor in my #safe_haven, surrounded by stone. #vss365

#WitchyFinds I found this piece of driftwood on the shores of Lake Superior at Grand Portage around 25 years ago. I added the crystal, which I bought at a metaphysical store called The Black Unicorn, and it’s been my wand ever since.
Hollow Rock, Grand Portage 🌊 Lake Superior crashing, sky brooding, trees clinging to stone. A quiet defiance in the arch—weathered and wild. #NorthShoreMN #HollowRock #NaturePhotography #LakeSuperior
My (then) five year old handed me my (our) pet trilobite as we were collecting fun rocks on the shore of Lake Huron. Thanks for the back story!

Over the next 4 years, I will be looking for #MichiganErratics that we can date for an #NSF #Geology #research project to reexamine the retreat history of the Laurentide Ice Sheet from the #GreatLakes! 1286 letters to landowners sent and 989 more going out today! If you know a big rock, let me know!

Here are two halves of a porcelaneous Datolite double nodule specimen from the Keweenaw Peninsula of Michigan. Self-collected many years ago. (approx. size 1.5" x 1.125") #lakesuperior, #michigan, #puremichigan, #rocks&minerals, #datolite, #photography, #yooper, #copperminerals, #upperpeninsula
I want to rock hunt on the shores of the Great Lakes so bad!

Surprise rock check? Okay, here’s some I grabbed off the southern shore of Lake Erie a few weeks ago. There’s such a fun mix of rock types there, you can have a whole geology lecture on the beach! If I had time in undergrad I would’ve gotten a geology degree, I was only a few classes short

Porcelain with Lake Superior clay and stone with wood ash glaze.
.
Jonathan Walburg of Lake Superior Pottery. I use many local materials to make unique pottery with a look, feel and story distinct to The Greatest Lake. Love a place and take it home to use everyday.

#RockinTuesday Still maybe my favorite of all hashtag days. Here is one of mine. Effervescent rocks? Carbonated rocks? Bubbly rocks? Call it what you will, Lake Superior shore rocks surrounded with air bubbles.
BRB vaporizing Lake Superior so I can watch Night of the Hunter in HDR

#RockinTuesday
Keeping the rocks local… the beach at Fair Haven State Park NY on the south shore of Lake Ontario
I wanna go to the Great Lakes to go look at/find rocks so baddddd
Background info, I grew up in Michigan and did a lot of rock hunting by the Great Lakes as a kid, so this is extra special. 🥰

I found "diamonds" along the Lake Michigan shoreline today, part of me wanted to pick them up and take them all home but alas...they would have melted in my car
#winter #photography #LakeMichigan
Hollow Rock, a natural wonder just off the north shore of Lake Superior near Grand Portage, MN. Formed by centuries of splashing waves hollowing out the center of the arch.

Good Morning.
Just a cool rock I found a few years ago on the shore of Lake Superior near Thunder Bay.

Went to a beach in Michigan to look for rocks the other day, and everyone else there was also looking for rocks. 🥺

In Northern Michigan vacationing. First time up here, staying right on Lake Michigan, like 100 yards from the water, and they’ve got the best rocks on the beaches here. Infinite supply of cool rocks. Huge variety of colors, lots of fossil shards, most smoothed down by millennia gently churning

Very nice! I've found a couple of similar pieces – basalt from the North Shore of Lake Superior here in Minnesota, USA. But yours has lots more amygdules and I like how it looks like an image from deep space with lots of stars and galaxies!
Porcelain with a Lake Superior iron with wood ash glaze.
Spent the weekend perching on rocks trying not to fall into the lake with my fancy nerd outfit. #larp #conquestofvastarlands #oc #rpg
Clear waters of Lake Ontario with my precious rocks beneath. #rocks #rockhounding #beachrocks #lakeontario #greatlakes #stones #pebbles #water #watersedge #april
my true form, scavenging things from places. this time: the beach. i found some Cool Rocks™ #instagram #genevaonthelake #lakeerie #summer
I borrowed the library's metal detector and took my cousins mudlarking in the industrial Great Lakes shoreline. Here's an hours worth of treasures:

Sculptural coil basketry experiments continue! I was going for a wavy/Great Lakes color and shape here, but that didn’t lend itself to a very sturdy structure. However, putting one of my many lake rocks into it shows off its stretchy waves! 🎨 #coilbasketry #sculpturalbasketry #greatlakes

Someone asked where I get the rocks I tumble. I do buy some, but I also pick up rocks (found some pretty jaspers while in New Mexico, for example) when traveling and also go rockhunting up at Lake Michigan sometimes.

So cool!!
Do y’all collect fossils?
What are some of the favorites you have?
I like these two that have a bunch of little guys in them. Pretty sure found these along Lake Michigan?

Thomsonite is an all-time MN classic! ✨ It's a rare zeolite mineral that (like most cool rocks) is the product of volcanic activity. 🌋 This variety started to form over 600 million years ago and can only be found on a limited stretch of the Lake Superior shoreline. 🌊 #mineralogy #geology #thomsonite

It's #RockinTuesday time! 𝘉𝘦𝘢𝘤𝘩𝘤𝘰𝘮𝘣𝘪𝘯𝘨 𝘉𝘳𝘪𝘭𝘭𝘪𝘢𝘯𝘤𝘦 - output from the largest rock tumbler on the continent. #Photography
#NorthShore
#LakeSuperior
#SouthOfTheBorder

#Nature

May have told this tale here before: I got them, as well as all the Corum, Runestaff and Swords books, for $25 in a secondhand bookshop in the little town on the shore of Lake Huron we go on holiday to every year. The next year I was so excited to go back in and see what new jewels they had for me.
Shenanigans were had at Lakeport, MI. Cho added a new location to his visit list! Lots of pretty rocks were collected.

It’s called Yooperlight and it’s found on the Great Lakes beaches AND IT GLOWS ORANGE UNDER UV LIGHT. They were discovered in 2017 SO RELATIVELY NEW ROCK (I mean the rock isn’t new obviously but I love when humans discover “new” things) YAY ROCKS

Anyway, here’s the marker about the rarely occurring geologic formation, topped with Niagara Limestone, popular with pelicans, plus more pelican pics from Sheboygan’s Lake Michigan shoreline on a foggy day. 2/2
This past weekend at camp on Lake Ontario. Found a rock with a sweet little heart shaped divot in it. 💙

Some great fossil finds on Lake Michigan today. It was beautiful out!
#rocks #rocksky #fossils #minerals
Ceramic tile of big waves on Minnesota’s North Shore. Probably my favorite piece from my latest ceramic class.

Yay! First one I found has to be my kid's, because I don't keep painted rocks. Next is some petrified wood that was a gift years ago. The 2 doorway rocks are from a beach on the keewenaw along lake Superior.

Jonathan Walburg of Lake Superior Pottery uses local materials to make unique pottery with a look, feel and story distinct to The Great Lake. It is porcelain with Lake Superior’s rocks, sand and clay with a wood ash glaze.

Day 5: 🪨🪨🪨🪨🪨🪨🪨🪨🪨🪨 The first complete set of #MichiganErratics for my #NSF research project constraining Laurentide #IceSheet retreat through the #GreatLakes 1 down, 7 to go! #Geology #EarthScience #Research #Michigan

Flooding in Northern Michigan. 1604 today on the Maple River near Burt Lake, Emmet County MI. Water up to bottom of Brutus Rd bridge, but no visible damage to the timber bridge built in 2016. Drains into Cheboygan River watershed. .bsky.social #miwx #floods #GreatLakes #climate #water
Dark blue is the most common color for Sodalite. It comes in a variety of colors. This is Yooperlite found near or on the beaches of the Great Lakes. Fun to hunt for at dusk with a UV light.
Spied a heart-shaped rock earlier this year on the south shore of Lake Michigan

Another interesting thing about this Lake Michigan sand is that a small minority of the grains are fluorescent. Here on this 40x field w/ a UV light, most grains just reflect some of the visible violet light, but one fluoresces. I noticed this when I was scanning a pinch of sand I’d collected (1/9)
I can participate in #RockinTuesday today. I remembered I had a picture of this big rock that sits along the shore of Lake Erie.

This is a rock that I collected myself! It is a piece of banded iron, I believe from the Middle Vulcan Iron Formation in Michigan. I found it on the shore of Lake Superior in Minnesota. I need to find a lapidary to cut a flat face on to it so it can be polished, to really show off it's features
Lake Superior Rocks (Sold a print of this yesterday) danielmickphotography.smugmug.com/Prints/Lake-...

Hollow Rock, a natural wonder just off the north shore of Lake Superior in Grand Portage MN. Over the centuries the arch has been hollowed out by crashing and splashing waves, hollowing out the center. More info/options here: tom-halseth.pixels.com/featured/hol...
Hi! New here! Just a few rocks from my collection of rocks! #Rockhound These came from Upper Michigan area, Lake Superior
Stones adorn the shoreline of Lake Superior somewhere deep along the shoreline of Michigan’s Porcupine Mountains. Thank you for looking everyone, have an absolutely outstanding Thursday!

Update: I dug up two more small hostas today. Also took a few rocks from the yard. My mom hauled so many rocks home from the north shore of Lake Superior and used them all over as part of her landscaping. The rest of us hunted for agates, she'd look for good shapes & colors.

A couple years ago we went hunting for agates on the shores of Lake Superior. Lily came with us. When she noticed that we were picking up rocks, tossing some back, and keeping a few, she picked up a rock of her own. She kept dropping it but always went back for the same one. We saved that one.

I dunno how to use this app. Here’s a rock from my collection that I cabbed.

Here's a nice little translucent specimen of porcelaneous Datolite from the Keweenaw Peninsula of Michigan. Self-collected. (approx. size .75" x .5") #lakesuperior, #michigan, #puremichigan, #rocks&minerals, #datolite, #photography, #yooper, #copperminerals, #upperpeninsula
I needed to find a pet rock for our Summer Reading Club. My friend is at the Great Lakes and she sends me this message. I chose roundyboi on the bottom left.
Assorted rocks from Lake Michigan. I have recruited 9's tumbling services when we get home

Outstanding article (from 2019) about Thomsonite: I find it difficult at times to describe this gem & how cool & rare it is. Spent hundreds of hours in my youth on the shores of Lake Superior searching for this rock.
Small rocks from Lake Superior earlier this month, at least one of which may be an agate...maybe. .
The Lake Superior shoreline is full of pocket rocks
Flay lay. Porcelain with Lake Superior clay, sand, rock and ash glaze.

Loving this little 100% Lake Superior high fire clay with wood ash glaze. Cone 9/10 reduction. Nice trimming as the clay breaks at the tool’s edge showing lack of plasticity. Also noting its lack of iron in comparison to the abundant low fire red clay in the area. Love exploring ❤️

#rock, #rockhound, #rocktumbling This is my tunbling load after the ginal pllish, load 4, in 1200 aluminum grit.
It is slab saw scraps I bought many years ago, a few odd rocks, and a few Lake Superior rocks.
I did do a 2 hour Borax run yet after this photo was taken yet.
Rock of Ages Light in Lake Superior, built 1908:
i am offering this unripe mango of a person to lake superior as a gift and also adding "pox-carrying yarnhamite" to my epithets rolodex
The addiction is strong. Some people are getting rocks in their stockings instead of lumps of Coal .🫣🫣🫣 All gathered along the shores of Lake Huron 🇨🇦 side. See one you Like ?
Neat rocks at Agate Beach, Rogers City, along Michigan's Lake Huron shore

With many steps omitted, here in four image is a medieval #knight #woodcarving that I made. Had the branch I chose been a few inches longer the knight would have been taller. The green jewel in the shield is #seaglass, something I picked up on the northern shore of Lake Ontario, #Toronto

No, I’m not testing #MichiganErratics for allergies. Dots are #SchmidtHammer measurements that reveal the relative hardness of #rock surfaces. Strong rock ➡️ short exposure; weak rock ➡️ long exposure. #geomorphology #geology #geochronology #GreatLakes

I have OPTIONS for this project. The rocks might not look like much but beneath them mountains of uranium waste are leaking into the groundwater. The map is the world's largest methane cloud. Lots of fun to fix with your space heater, I promise!
Found a mystery rock on the beach of Lake Michigan
can't believe I went all the way to lake superior yesterday and didn't bring my black light to look for yooperlite. felt like a real dumbass at the beach
lots of basalt is visible along the shoreline of lake superior, little reefs of it just offshore along M-26
A cool looking rock I found while snowshoeing on Lake Superior.

#rockhound
#rockpolishing Tumbler load 1 is ready for Polish! I had given it an extra week with the rough grit. I just put it in for a few hours of cleaning with Borax first.Most of these are scraps from a slab cutter I bought years ago. A few (3 on bottom right I think) are from Lake Superior.

This specimen has been in .bsky.social for nearly 130 years — but has only two minute labels reading "Marquette, Michigan" & "H.G.H." [Henry Guernsey Hubbard] — Hubbard collected extensively in the Lake Superior region in 1876 (Mallis 1971), making this specimens over 149 years old!

The "dragon egg rock" in the video is a real type called Yooperlite, a syenite rock with fluorescent sodalite that glows orange under UV light. Discovered in 2017 on Lake Superior beaches in Michigan, they're nicknamed dragon eggs for their fiery appearance.#Geology

i keep seeing stuff about weighted plushies, sorta contemplating trying to make one either with sand or like, rocks from a riverbank or the north shore of lake superior of the right size to see if i can at some point hmm

Apart from a bit of silliness I was out scouting for red pine dendro fire history sites along the shore of Lake Superior. And I was doing it wearing my late father's "shooting glasses" that I came across this past week.

You can read more in the alt, but I will literally break my back hunched over looking at rocks for hrs. -doesn’t matter if it’s a beach, landscaping, side of the road, etc…it looks like a Nazar! I’m in luv. And before the know-it-alls come for me. I’m 100% aware that an Agate isn’t a “rock.”🙄

Ok come with me for a little story. So in my town (I live in Michigan off Lake Huron) there is a fishing pier that has canals/wetland (probably an old overgrown boat launching sites since it’s next to the old marina) on either side of the road. On one side there is a very large rock… (1)
Using a branch I picked up along the northern shore of Lake Ontario, #Toronto #CANADA, I carved this elephant head #woodcarving #sculpture. #art #crafts #woodworking
Dremel polishing a rock the other day #rockhound #lgbt #smallbusiness #ftm #granite #lakeerie #unakite #greatlakes

Ever heard of a rock that looks good enough to eat? 🪨 Join us for a virtual event all about rockhounding in the Great Lakes! We're focusing on puddingstones with Madeline Sigler from the MSU Department Of Earth and Environmental Sciences See you June 25 at 7 PM ET: youtube.com/live/nAmQbSk...
#fossils most I find along the Lake Michigan shore.

#MineralMonday
Rocks in road grate; Beaver Island, Lake Michigan.

The specimen from Iowa is rather wimpy looking but then I'm a bit biased.
I once spotted a boulder about 4 feet diameter, the surface of which was entirely Hexagonaria.
Seen from a kayak on Lake Michigan, it was about 20 feet down in a field of boulders just off the foot of Sleeping Bear Sand Dunes.

Huge swathes of the central US where a lot of the intensive ag is done are underlain by carbonate rocks, so quite a bit of lime is quarried relatively nearby. Whereas you'd have to ship the basalt from the coasts or Lake Superior.
There are >100 ag lime quarries in Iowa, I can't find any basalt.

📷 Tarah Nicole Hoffman Found mostly around Lake Superior, these glowing rocks are known as Yooperlites. They are actually syanite rocks rich in fluorescent sodalite - and were only discovered recently!
I wanted to bring this rock home but it was wider than the narrow path down to the shore of Lake Superior. Plus I could not have gotten both the kid and the rock in the car.
Found a use for some of my small drilled rocks. Lake Superior Christmas ornaments.
Looks like Lake Superior shoreline. Good rock hunting!

Naw they are from whidbey island.
I've seen there's some very cool rocks to be had around lake superior
Brb hunting for glowing rocks on Lake Superior
Lake Superior rocks just like I remember credit: Shawn Malone, Lake Superior Photo - Marquette
Found this agate yesterday on the shore of Lake Superior. I really love it. 😊 The colors are rare and so beautiful. .bsky.social

WELL I went to lake superior recently and I wanna wrap some of the stones I found. And then I went on a tiktok rabbit hole and I realized I have everything but wire to make a shit ton of pretty jewelry 😌 im SO excited LMAO
Super cool yooperlite I found in Lake Superior
Today, I went rock hunting at Lake Superior!

Yeah, all my good rock hunting was in the West and Southwest. Can’t even think of a single stone (other than Pennsylvania slate) I’ve ever collected from the East. (I do have some contraband from Bad River Res on Lake Superior that I’m planning to return. I didn’t know!)

My first trip to look for agates on Lake Superior was in 1957, I was 2 years old and I still have an indelible mind picture of the windy rock beach near Grand Marais, Michigan, along the southern shore.
I know little of formal geology but as an artist know how to work the material.
I'd like to place a claim on Lake Superior granite
Reminds me of the ancient rock of the Lake Superior shoreline.

I found a pudding stone! These rocks are so cool. Named after their resemblance to English pudding. They are characterized by a number of different rocks seemingly encased in concrete or aggregate. Geology rocks! #LakeHuron
#Tobermory
#geology

An assemblage made of flotsam from Lake Ontario, twigs and an acorn cap from a trail beer my house, wolf fur from my old Yukon parka, ribbon from the Dollar Store
an sundries. 🇨🇦🇨🇦🇨🇦🦫
Googly eyes found randomly painted on a rock on the Red Hill Creek hiking trail, near the end of the line, about a km from Lake Ontario.

I was walking the shore of Lake Ontario near Ft. Drum with family and the shale was full of fossils. Probably prosaic to an expert, but amazing to me - shells, something spidery - and I was creating stacks of rocks as I went to take back later.
I spent part of my day along the shore of Lake Ontario looking for Hag’s stones. No luck, but the fresh air coming off the lake cleared my cluttered head. #hagstone #lakeontario #magic

This rock had a number of fossils. Looking forward to a possible trip to Upper Michigan to look for rocks along Superior and other adventures. I'm hoping for a possible Arthur M Anderson sighting. #greatlakes

One of the best rocks I found on the trip was this floating rock. I know there are volcanic rocks along the Great Lakes, but I thought mostly along Lake Superior, and further north. Felt very light for its size size when I picked it up. On a beach, Manistee, MI. (Video at the birdsite).

I totally didn’t see that you are a geologist. My apologies for explaining what you already know. I’m just a rockhound that lives in the Great Lakes and have had the pleasure of doing deep dives into the sodalite in the area and then some. Again, I didn’t mean to undermine your area of expertise.
I made a map for sodalite in the Great Lakes about 3 years ago with over 72 locations. You can find the map on my website: exploremorenorthamerica.com
I really like Granite. Love Henderson and bandit and Great Lakes etc.

I asked my mom to bring me some rocks from the Great Lakes so that'll be fun

i was kinda waiting for your rock.. haha. this is is awesome. and of course a 'great lake'.. and i adore my great lakes (especially ontario). but was counting on you, .bsky.social and .bsky.social...expecting interest or actual rock pics from.. and nailed it basically.. 😂

Great lake rocks are just... Great. I have an unassuming rock in my car that everyone is drawn too because it's somehow both black and a powder blue. And whenever I tell folks it's from Lake Michigan everyone gets stoked.
I wish! This lot I bought from Kingsley North. BUT, I have two tumblers full of rocks I collected myself (on Lake Michigan) going now, so I am excited to see those.
I brought back Lake Michigan rocks for a small project ❤️

This reminds me: I found a gold necklace with a '6' charm on it last week buried in the sand at Bohemian Beach near Glen Arbor, Michigan while 18mo and I were digging for rocks to stack. It was promptly re-lost, apparently fated to remain on the shore of Lake Michigan.

Uncle Dave is a geologist and tells me more than I ever want to know about the rocks we find on the shore of Lake Michigan. He also lived next door to John Mellencamp's drummer, so he heard all their music before it came out, because even after they got famous, they practiced in the garage.
I think rock hunting on Lake Michigan is the most Midwest activity humanly possible.

I don't know why this picture comes out upside down--these are the rocks (the big ones) I collected from the Lake Michigan shore in Goodhart in the 80s when we rented a cottage on the lake. The movers dumped them there on top of the rock landscaping (yes, when we moved, I made them move rocks). 1/2
I love seeing part of my Grandpa’s rock and mineral collection on display. I have very fond memories of rock hunting (primarily Lake Superior Agate) with both of my grandparents.

At the beginning of the Covid-19 pandemic, I went to a beach on Lake Superior (where Sylvia Plath once spent a weekend camping!) and found a triangular piece of weathered sandstone. I told the rock I'd bring it back to the beach again if I survived covid. I returned the rock the two springs later.

This green rock is a big chunk of epidote and prehnite about a billion years old the replaced the crevices in basalt at extreme motherfucking heat. Tumbled for several million years until it became the perfect natural palm stone I found in Lake Superior. IN Lake Superior bc I dove underwater for it.

Anyone remember the hidden marble walkway just east of Marble Island? I'd love to know why it was under the ocean and what plans they had for it. It made for a great secret duel pit on Lake Superior! Any idea .bsky.social? #UO #RetroGaming #Storytelling
Thanks! Few bits and pieces I’ve had for ages. Not sure how piecing a binding will go with all the seams it will have! Those are my pattern weights, Lake Superior beach rocks.
Can confirm the rocks at Lake Superior rock. Treasures! 💙 this video.
Some asmr of rock hunting on Lake Superior bc life is stressful and rocks are pretty.

Also, for anyone interested in going to look for rocks on Lake Superior, the glowing rocks are found in Newberry. We stayed in Grand Marais and could walk to the beach, great for camping. You’ll need a UV flashlight. Super small town (like their entire K-12 is only 36 kids.) but really nice people.
At first I thought the Antelope pin was a Lake Superior donut pin, which would be pretty cool
What we are looking at??? Diatoms, Rock Pool in North Shore of Lake Superior
I went whenever I could to Lake Superior and the Keweenaw Peninsula, the remains of billion years old mountains rich with copper, silver and brilliant gemstones.

Garden Rock #3. Chunk of slate from north shore of Lake Superior. Wave patterns indicate what this former silt/ mud would have looked like on a windy & wavy day on the shores of an ancient sea 1 billion years ago. #gardensky

The rocks with Mica in them come from a small mica mine about an hour south of Bancroft. The beach stone from Lake Huron near my hometown. Been collecting them since I was a kid. Just talked to a friend who has now started collecting them down around the Bowmanville area. Yes that's a rock.
General call for pictures of cool rocks. This is one I found at Lake Huron that is perfectly shaped to hold a stick of incense
We went seaglass hunting along the Lake Huron shores last week.

Some of my favorite rocks from across the street. 400,000,000 year old fossils dredged up by glaciers and churned around in Lake Huron for millenia. Then shined up even more in my tumbler that I gave to my BFF when she caught the bug during a visit. 🥰

I love this little heritage building. Dating from about 1840, it was built with Dundas shale pulled from Lake Ontario, just outside its door. I'm usually walking my dog when I pass it & don't usually get time to line up a good shot but managed to get one today. This corner shows off the shale. ❤️

Maybe I’ll do a series of garden rock pics - I love placing rocks with plants, because I like geology and as a reminder of places I’ve been. This rock, scraped by glacial activity, was falling out of a 25-m tall bank of glaciolacustrine clay on the shore of Lake Ontario.
I have (old) rocks. #GeologySky Fossil crinoid columnal (ossicle) #LakeMichigan #GreatLakes

I just think of all the fantastic rocks that wash up in the Great Lakes from various rock layers across the whole basin, it would be fun to ID them and see if I can’t match them up to their original layer

The oldest rock on earth, Acasta Gneiss, is on the N shore of Lake Superior. Other than grad school and a couple of jobs, I lived in MN and visited and camped often in Northern Minnesota/Great Lakes. Love it!
I love to walk our Great Lakes and find rocks for my garden and pond area. 💙👋

indeed. i need to go to the great lakes and go rock hunting soon. my friend does a yearly trip where they walk along beaches and collect cool rocks. they always bring me some river rocks, but i want to get some of my own! but they are gorgeous to look at! and just so cool to have!

A – Anishinaabe Birchbark Box
This decorated box features porcupine quillwork, a traditional Anishinaabe craft. Near the Great Lakes, clay is scarce, but birchbark is abundant—and can be harvested without harming trees. These containers are lightweight, airtight, and even waterproof!

A native Midwesterner, I first saw it when I was 24, and remember thinking that pretty much every rural Michigan citizen has a rock this size, coughed up by the glaciers that scooped the Great Lakes, at the foot of their long driveway.

Gorgeous! I love hunting for fossilized/agatized coral and pretty rocks (Lake Michigan, not ocean) and have always wanted to find a way to use my finds. Literally every counter in the house has a mason jar full of “momma’s rocks.” 😂🤣 I think they’re getting sick of my treasures.

My dad and I were playing in lake Michigan throwing rocks and trying to make big splashes when he found what we assume is a cephalopod/nautilus fossil. Pretty sick stuff. I was OBSESSED with dinosaurs and paleontology at the time so that was a fucking miracle to me. So cool.

That’s so cool! Rocks are amazing :D For more context, Lake Michigan, Chicago-side. Some sleuthing tells me it’s similar to other fossilized rocks people have found in the area that are more obvious, but too hard to truly tell. Regardless, had a blast!

Yes! I found a tiny puddingstone, some coral-like fossils, and some lovely purple-gray quartz. I have been wanting to go back so bad! I watch people on YouTube rock hunting in Lake Michigan all the time.
Oh those hearts. They are among some of my prized possessions. Some hearts are also from the magnificent glacial rocks of Lake Michigan and Lake Superior.
Hell yeah my mom brought me back some rocks from Lake Michigan ✊ now I just gotta identify em lol
Beautiful, I'm a rock hound and love rock hunting the N.W. Lake Michigan area.

😂 Northern Lake Michigan from Mackinac to Esky and all the way down the bay of Green Bay is one giant sand bar. We have some rocks but they don't look too attractive. I go to Lake Superior and its volcanic past for good rocks lol

Magnetite coming out of the dunes on Lake Michigan. There's some gold in there too.

It's even moreso IRL. This iron slag comes from one shore area along Lake Michigan that's not far from Traverse City, where they have the cherry blossom festival every year. This always make me think of that.

Near my house is Lake Erie. There's a long path along the shore, lots of driftwood and assorted rocks, stones and pebbles, and people find amazing things to do with them. Favorite, so far - long pieces of driftwood balanced on rocks, each other, with more rocks balanced on the driftwood.
My most recent cool rock is black shale from Point Pelee, Lake Erie. It's both flat and ovoid. 😁 Very smooth.

Slip is visible between Canal Park and the Arena-Auditorium. Off the shore of Canal Park in Lake Superior are the remnants of the Whitney rock crushing enterprise, visible here as a small brown rectangle. The Whitney Brothers, of Superior, Wisconsin, had a sand and gravel processing business ...

I think "Dryberry" is the translation from the Ojibway name for the lake. If you had assumed that we discovered all the rock types, you'd be wrong. In 2017, a guy randomly took a black light out to the shores of lake Michigan, and stumbled upon a new type of syenite rock. It appears unremarkable but under the black light, it glows orange and pink and green.
Orthocone fossil I found on the shoreline of Lake Michigan yesterday. I’m hoping there’s a chance I can clean this up a bit and reveal more of what’s underneath!
I love opuntia. I've been trying to get some from a blueberry farm near Lake Michigan. Yours looks well established. So pretty.

My dad collected gems & minerals. Growing up, we’d take at least one trip each year to the north shore of Lake Superior to hunt for agates & thomsonite mostly. He’d go to “rock shows” (thus my joking about rocking on). Some rocks from the drawers.

Outstanding article (from 2019) about Thomsonite: I find it difficult at times to describe this I gem & how cool & rare it is. Spent hundreds of hours in my youth on the shores of Lake Superior searching for this rock.
